title: Writer-Reviewer
description: Writer-reviewer is a dual-role factory technique: a writer produces a candidate, and a reviewer accepts or rejects it.

whatItIs.title: What It Is
whatItIs.body: Writer-reviewer is a dual-role factory technique: one role writes or executes, and another role reviews. The writer (or executor) produces a candidate—a draft, change set, or other deliverable. The reviewer then accepts that candidate to complete the goal, or rejects it and sends the work back for another write pass. Completion is gated on review, not on the first write finishing.

whyItMatters.title: Why It Matters
whyItMatters.body: Quality-gated agent work can keep revising until review accepts, instead of treating the first candidate as final. Reject cycles stay intentional: each reject returns work for another write pass under the same goal, rather than ending after one chat reply. That makes review a real gate on completion, not an optional afterthought.

howItWorks.title: How It Works
howItWorks.body: Write or execute produces a candidate. Review accepts to complete or rejects back to write. Bound reject retries with a loop breaker so cycles cannot run forever.

comparedToNearbyTechniques.title: Compared To Nearby Techniques
comparedToNearbyTechniques.body: This technique glossary page defines writer-reviewer in isolation. It is not the write-review use-case guide, not a plain factory loop without a review gate, and not planner-executor or worker-adviser by default.
